Green controller rotates in the opposite direction!

sections.content: Green rotates right (CW), red rotates left (CCW). Now let me understand the goal by examining all tracks and trying rotations. Let me check if the top track also rotates.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the top track's red controller position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
r_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R' and n['pixels'] == 10]
print("R (red) controller blocks:")
for n in r_blocks:
    bd = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in bd) / len(bd)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in bd) / len(bd)
    print(f"  center ({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f})")

# Top track red center: (18, 20)
# Let me try rotating the top track
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 20}])

print(f"\nBoard changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', 'unknown')}")

# Compare before/after for top track
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTop track row 17:")
print(f"  Before: {before[17][14:56]}")
print(f"  After:  {after[17][14:56]}")

# Also check middle and bottom tracks
print("\nMiddle track row 26:")
print(f"  Before: {before[26][14:56]}")
print(f"  After:  {after[26][14:56]}")

print("\nBottom track row 35:")
print(f"  Before: {before[35][14:56]}")
print(f"  After:  {after[35][14:56]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

# Let me check the top track's red controller position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
r_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R' and n['pixels'] == 10]
print("R (red) controller blocks:")
for n in r_blocks:
    bd = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in bd) / len(bd)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in bd) / len(bd)
    print(f"  center ({cr:.0f}, {cc:.0f})")

# Top track red center: (18, 20)
# Let me try rotating the top track
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 20}])

after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
